title: Add per-row exception handling for parse_utc in get_recent_runs
analysis: In get_recent_runs (reconciliation.py lines 80-83), the guard `if row['completed_at'] is not None` only protects against NULL completed_at. If started_at is NULL while completed_at is not NULL, parse_utc(row['started_at']) raises TypeError. This TypeError propagates through _query and with_db (which only catches sqlite3.OperationalError), so the caller gets an unhandled exception instead of the declared default []. The fix is to wrap the duration computation in a per-row try/except (ValueError, TypeError) matching the existing pattern in partition_burst_state (lines 46-55) and get_burst_state (lines 269-276). The test should insert a row with NULL started_at and non-NULL completed_at and verify duration_seconds is None without raising. A secondary test for malformed (non-ISO) started_at is also warranted since it exercises the ValueError branch.

steps.description: Write failing test: test_null_started_at_with_completed_at_sets_duration_none — insert a run row with started_at=NULL (via direct SQL bypassing NOT NULL since SQLite is lenient) and completed_at set to a valid timestamp. Call get_recent_runs and assert the run is returned with duration_seconds=None and no exception raised. Also verify a DEBUG log mentioning 'bad timestamps' is emitted.

Wrap the duration computation block in get_recent_runs (lines 80-83) in a try/except (ValueError, TypeError) that sets duration=None and logs logger.debug('run %s: bad timestamps (%s)', row['id'], exc), matching the partition_burst_state pattern.

Write test: test_malformed_started_at_with_completed_at_sets_duration_none — insert a run with started_at='not-a-timestamp' and a valid completed_at. Assert duration_seconds=None, no exception, and DEBUG log emitted. This exercises the ValueError branch of the same try/except.

Write test: test_null_started_at_does_not_affect_other_rows — insert two runs: one with NULL started_at + valid completed_at, one with valid started_at + valid completed_at. Assert the healthy row still has correct duration_seconds and the bad row has duration_seconds=None. This verifies per-row isolation (bad row doesn't abort the entire query).

design_decisions.rationale: parse_utc raises TypeError for None input and ValueError for malformed strings. Both are possible data corruption scenarios that should degrade gracefully. This matches the exact exception tuple used in partition_burst_state and get_burst_state.

design_decisions.decision: Log at DEBUG level, not WARNING
design_decisions.rationale: Matches the logging level used in partition_burst_state (line 52) and get_burst_state (line 274). Dashboard data layer treats bad timestamps as debug-level noise, not operational warnings.

design_decisions.decision: Set duration=None on error rather than skipping the row
design_decisions.rationale: The row is still valid data — only the computed duration is unreliable. Callers expect all runs to appear in the result list. Setting duration_seconds=None is the same value used for incomplete runs (no completed_at), so consumers already handle it.

design_decisions.decision: Test via direct SQL INSERT with NULL started_at despite schema NOT NULL constraint
design_decisions.rationale: SQLite does not enforce NOT NULL on TEXT columns the same way other databases do — NULLs can appear via direct inserts, migrations, or schema changes. The task explicitly describes this scenario occurring in production data.

reuse.how: Copy the same pattern: try/except around parse_utc calls, set fallback value on error, emit logger.debug with row identifier and exception

reuse.what: RECONCILIATION_SCHEMA constant for test DB setup
reuse.how: Import from tests.conftest and use with sqlite3.connect + executescript to create ad-hoc test databases with custom data

reuse.what: Inline tmp_path DB creation pattern for edge-case tests
reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: create DB in tmp_path, insert specific rows, open aiosqlite connection with Row factory, call the function under test
